title: ORM Patterns (Active Record vs Data Mapper)

card.intuition: The two primary ways of mapping code objects to database tables — either the object 'knows' how to save itself, or a separate service handles the saving.
card.analogy: Ordering pizza: Active Record is like a smart pizza that can drive itself to your house and put itself in the oven. Data Mapper is like you calling a delivery service to fetch and handle the pizza for you.
when_to_use: Active Record: Quick prototyping, simple CRUD apps, or small teams (e.g., Django, Rails)
Data Mapper: Complex domain logic, large-scale systems where DB schema and Code models must evolve separately (e.g., SQLAlchemy, TypeORM, Hibernate)
gotchas: Active Record couples your business logic to the database structure, making it harder to test without a real DB
Data Mapper is more verbose and has a steeper learning curve

exercise.prompt: Identify the ORM pattern based on the code style.
exercise.test_code:
assert identify_pattern("user.save()") == "Active Record"
assert identify_pattern("repo.save(user)") == "Data Mapper"
print("PASS")
